medications.drug_name: mechanical haemostasis (firm direct pressure + protective non-adherent dressing)
medications.indication_rationale: Control the bleeding from the avulsed base; keep the dressing intact/dry ~3 days; in-person cautery if needed.

medications.drug_name: elective removal (cryotherapy/shave) of irritated seborrhoeic keratoses — via GP
medications.indication_rationale: Optional removal of lesions that repeatedly catch/bleed, done electively after a skin check.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the dressing dry and intact for ~3 days, don't pull off the remaining lesion, and pat (not rub) the back when drying.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a very common, harmless 'age spot' (seborrhoeic keratosis) that tore and bled because it sits on the skin surface — made a bit worse by your aspirin. It doesn't sound like a burst melanoma.

Because I can't examine it with a dermatoscope over video, please have a routine in-person skin check in the next week or two to confirm it's benign — and any spot that changes, grows or bleeds on its own should be checked/biopsied.
Keep the dressing dry, press firmly for 15 minutes if it bleeds again, and go to ED if bleeding won't stop or the area becomes hot, red and swollen.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Dermoscopic confirmation of the benign diagnosis, healing of the avulsed base, and a full skin check; elective removal of irritating lesions.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Uncontrolled bleeding despite 15 minutes of firm pressure, or a hot/red/swollen infected base — seek urgent/emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise likely benign seborrhoeic keratosis from morphology/stability while keeping melanoma on the differential
Arrange a routine in-person dermoscopic skin check (cannot dermoscope remotely) — no absolute remote reassurance
Mechanical haemostasis (firm pressure, protective dressing) and note the aspirin effect
Skin-check/biopsy safety-net for any changing lesion
acceptable_alternatives: Elective cryotherapy/shave removal of irritated lesions after a skin check
errors_of_omission: No in-person skin-check pathway to confirm benignity
errors_of_commission: Absolute remote reassurance without dermoscopy (missing melanoma)
ED over-escalation for a benign mechanically-bled lesion
Advising the patient to pull off the remaining lesion
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires proportionate reassurance + a routine in-person skin check to confirm benignity (not absolute remote reassurance, not ED), plus haemostasis advice.
